macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Jobs Added 157,000 in July, Below Expectations; Unemployment Holds at 3.9%
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 157,000 jobs in July, falling short of analyst forecasts, while the unemployment rate remained steady at 3.9%, reinforcing concerns about a rebounding hiring market pressuring the Fed on interest rates[3].

items.title: Fed Rate Hike Narrative Intensifies After Hot CPI, Payrolls, and Hawkish Stance
items.summary: Following strong April CPI, hot May non-farm payrolls, and a hawkish Federal Reserve, market sentiment now suggests the economy may overheat further, potentially forcing the Fed to raise interest rates soon[1].

items.title: Kevin Warsh Appointed New Fed Chair; Market Bets on No Hikes This Year
items.summary: Kevin Warsh has been appointed as the new Federal Reserve chair, with analysts suggesting a safe bet on no interest rate hikes this year despite elevated prices and potential demand destruction[4].

items.title: Tesla Sales Surge in Europe Amid Price Cuts; U.S. Sales Decline
items.summary: Tesla rebounded in Europe during Q2 thanks to price cuts, offsetting declines in the U.S. market, signaling a recovery in international electric vehicle demand[2].

items.title: Egg Producers Pay $3.3M to Settle Federal Price-Fixing Suit; Donate 53M Eggs
items.summary: Egg producers agreed to a $3.3 million settlement in a federal price-fixing lawsuit and will donate over 53 million eggs to food banks across 17 states[2].

items.title: IMF Slows Global Growth Forecast to 2.9% in 2024 Amid Middle East War
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund revised its global economic growth forecast down to 2.9% for 2024, citing ongoing war in the Middle East and policy challenges[6].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs to $62,573 as Crypto Market Cap Reaches $2.17 Trillion
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 1.96% to $62,573 and Ethereum gained 3.34% to $1,758, pushing the total crypto market cap up 2.14% to $2.17 trillion, while XRP and Solana also saw significant weekly gains of over 8% and 16% respectively[1][4].

items.title: $184 Million in Crypto Liquidations Reflect Market Volatility
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced significant volatility over the past 24 hours, resulting in $184 million in total liquidations, with $88.2 million in long positions and $96.1 million in short positions wiped out[2].

items.title: Senator Gillibrand Proposes Ban on Officials Issuing Personal Crypto Tokens
items.summary: Senator Gillibrand introduced a proposal to ban government officials from issuing or sponsoring personal cryptocurrency tokens, raising regulatory risks for token launches, fundraising, and CEX/DeFi adoption[1].

items.title: Global Insurance Broker Aon Tests Stablecoin Payments with Coinbase
items.summary: Aon, a global insurance broker, is piloting stablecoin payments for insurance premiums in a test involving Coinbase and Paxos, signaling growing institutional adoption of stablecoins[3].

items.title: Nasdaq and Kraken Launch Trading for Tokenized Stocks
items.summary: Nasdaq and Kraken have teamed up to launch trading for tokenized stocks, a move analysts suggest could be aided by the U.S.'s limited exposure to oil shocks, which may be supporting Bitcoin's price[3].

items.title: Exchange Deposits Surge to Near 49,000 BTC Amid Cautious Sentiment
items.summary: Exchange deposits of Bitcoin neared 49,000 BTC, a level only hit four times in 2026, while market sentiment remains cautious with the Fear & Greed Index at 26 and the Altcoin Season Index at 48[1].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Flipkart Deploys 250+ AI Models, With 40% of Code Now AI-Written
items.summary: Walmart-owned Flipkart has deployed over 250 specialized AI models across its ecosystem, with AI now generating 35–40% of its software code, signaling a major shift in retail backend development.

items.title: Amazon Announces $13 Billion AI and Cloud Investment in India Through 2030
items.summary: Amazon revealed a staggering $13 billion investment in India to expand cloud and AI infrastructure, bringing its total planned investment in the country to $48 billion, while rival Meesho reports 70% of its code is AI-generated.

items.title: Yann LeCun Calls Elon Musk’s XAI a ‘Failure’ Amid Rebuilding Efforts
items.summary: AI pioneer Yann LeCun publicly criticized Elon Musk’s XAI as a failure, noting that Musk is rebuilding the company from the ground up after admitting the first version didn’t work, while rivals Anthropic and Google now rent Musk’s Colossus 1 cluster.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Breaks Internet in 24 Hours with Community-Built Mythos-Class Models
items.summary: The new Mythos-class model Claude Fable 5 has broken the internet within 24 hours, with the Claude community rapidly building innovative applications using the latest AI capabilities.

items.title: Tesla and SpaceX Positioning to Power the AI Revolution via Grid and Compute
items.summary: New research suggests Tesla’s energy infrastructure and SpaceX’s expanding AI compute business could unlock tens of gigawatts of AI data center capacity, potentially becoming the fastest-growing business in history.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Announces New PlayStation Controller and Multiple Game Reveals at State of Play
items.summary: Sony unveiled a new PlayStation controller with an unexpected design and revealed several major game announcements including Team Ninja's Nioh 3, Maze Theory's stealth franchise for VR, and ArtPlay's Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement during their June State of Play showcase.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals New Force of Law Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by announcing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond, a new expansion for the cooperative shooter that will soon be available to players.

items.title: GTA Online Weekly Update Features Triple Money and RP Bonuses
items.summary: GTA Online's latest weekly update includes triple money and triple RP on the stunt race series, double money and RP on bunker sales and ammunition contracts, offering players significant profit opportunities.

items.title: FURIA vs LYON G2 Highlights from LoL MSI 2026 Main Stage
items.summary: FURIA and LYON competed in the second game of their best-of-5 match at the League of Legends MSI 2026 Main Stage in Korea, with patch 26.13 and Season 16 gameplay.

items.title: GTA 6 Generates $3 Billion in 24 Hours Despite Ban Controversy
items.summary: GTA 6 achieved an unprecedented milestone by generating $3 billion in revenue within 24 hours of release, despite facing massive bans and controversy.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Generate $3 Billion in First 24 Hours
items.summary: GTA 6 achieved a record-breaking $3 billion in revenue within its first 24 hours of pre-orders, marking an unprecedented milestone for the gaming industry on PlayStation 5.

items.title: 1.7 Million Grilling Brushes Recalled Over Ingestion Hazard
items.summary: Conair voluntarily recalled 1.7 million Quezin Art grilling brushes with metal wires due to bristles detaching and entering food, with three reported cases of bristle ingestion.

items.title: National Mall Security Checkpoints Expanded for July 4th Celebrations
items.summary: For the first time, all visitors entering the National Mall must pass through security checkpoints including magnetometers amid heightened safety preparations for America's 250th birthday celebrations.
